hr.label: HR Interview
hr.description: Behavioral interview focused on workplace judgment, communication, motivation, and self-awareness.
hr.opening: Hi, welcome to your HR mock interview. I will ask adaptive behavioral questions and help you reflect without giving away scripted answers.

topics.name: Leadership
seed_question_intents: Ask about a time the candidate led without authority.
Ask about taking ownership of a difficult outcome.
Ask about making a decision with incomplete information.
topics.evaluation_rubric: Strong answers use a concrete situation, explain actions taken by the candidate, show trade-offs, and quantify or clearly describe impact.

topics.name: Teamwork
seed_question_intents: Ask about working with a teammate who had a different style.
Ask about helping a team meet an urgent goal.
Ask about receiving or giving feedback in a team.
topics.evaluation_rubric: Strong answers show collaboration, respect for others, practical communication, and a clear contribution to a shared result.

topics.name: Conflict Resolution
seed_question_intents: Ask about resolving a disagreement with a peer.
Ask about handling criticism from a manager or stakeholder.
Ask about balancing competing priorities across people.
topics.evaluation_rubric: Strong answers avoid blame, clarify both sides, explain the candidate's actions, and show a constructive resolution or learning.

topics.name: Communication
seed_question_intents: Ask about explaining a complex topic to a non-technical audience.
Ask about communicating bad news or project risk.
Ask about adapting communication for different stakeholders.
topics.evaluation_rubric: Strong answers are structured, audience-aware, concise, and show how the candidate checked for understanding.

topics.name: Strengths and Weaknesses
seed_question_intents: Ask about the candidate's greatest professional strength.
Ask about a weakness the candidate is actively improving.
Ask about feedback the candidate has received and acted on.
topics.evaluation_rubric: Strong answers are specific, credible, and show practical evidence rather than generic personality claims.

topics.name: Career Goals
seed_question_intents: Ask about why the candidate wants this role.
Ask about where the candidate wants to grow over the next few years.
Ask about the kind of work environment where the candidate thrives.
topics.evaluation_rubric: Strong answers connect the candidate's goals to the role, show curiosity, and avoid sounding unfocused or purely transactional.

technical_sde.label: Technical Interview (SDE)
technical_sde.description: Software engineering interview across CS fundamentals, coding reasoning, and system design thinking.
technical_sde.opening: Hi, welcome to your technical SDE mock interview. I will ask adaptive engineering questions, probe your reasoning, and offer hints when you get stuck.

topics.name: Object-Oriented Programming
seed_question_intents: Ask the candidate to explain OOP through design principles rather than definitions.
Ask about when inheritance is harmful and composition is preferable.
Ask about modeling a simple real-world domain with classes and interfaces.
topics.evaluation_rubric: Strong answers explain principles, include practical examples, and discuss design trade-offs instead of only naming terms.

topics.name: DBMS
seed_question_intents: Ask about how indexes improve reads and affect writes.
Ask about transactions and ACID properties in a real scenario.
Ask about choosing normalization or denormalization.
topics.evaluation_rubric: Strong answers explain correctness, performance, and trade-offs with concrete database examples.

topics.name: Operating Systems
seed_question_intents: Ask about process versus thread behavior.
Ask about deadlocks and prevention strategies.
Ask about concurrency issues and synchronization primitives.
topics.evaluation_rubric: Strong answers connect OS concepts to resource management, memory, concurrency, and practical system behavior.

topics.name: Networking
seed_question_intents: Ask what happens when a user enters a URL in a browser.
Ask about TCP versus UDP trade-offs.
Ask about handling unreliable networks in applications.
topics.evaluation_rubric: Strong answers trace request flow, identify protocols, and discuss reliability, latency, and security considerations.

topics.name: Java
seed_question_intents: Ask about HashMap behavior and collision handling.
Ask about checked versus unchecked exceptions.
Ask about Java concurrency primitives and thread safety.
topics.evaluation_rubric: Strong answers combine language mechanics with practical coding consequences and common pitfalls.

topics.name: Data Structures and Algorithms
seed_question_intents: Ask the candidate to choose a data structure for a realistic problem.
Ask about complexity trade-offs between two approaches.
Ask about recognizing when hashing, trees, or graphs fit a problem.
topics.evaluation_rubric: Strong answers reason about constraints, complexity, edge cases, and why a data structure or algorithm fits.

topics.name: System Design
seed_question_intents: Ask about designing a URL shortener, notification service, or feed service at a high level.
Ask about scaling reads and writes for a simple service.
Ask about trade-offs between consistency, availability, and latency.
topics.evaluation_rubric: Strong answers clarify requirements, identify core components, explain data flow, and discuss bottlenecks and trade-offs.
